text: The video demonstrates the construction of an S pouch for redo pouch surgery, highlighting its ability to extend further into the pelvis compared to a J pouch. The S pouch is formed using three limbs of terminal ileum, with seromuscular sutures approximating the ileal segments. An S-shaped enterotomy line is marked with a bovie, and stay sutures maintain orientation. Linear enterotomies are made with a tonsil or gloved finger, and 3-0 braided absorbable sutures are used for the posterior anastomotic lines. The anterior wall is closed with continuous seromuscular sutures, followed by an insufflation test with saline to check staple line integrity.
